nodes.name: Slack Trigger
nodes.notes: Listens for new messages in #infra-issues channel. Replace YOUR_CHANNEL_ID with actual channel ID.

nodes.name: Filter Bot Messages
nodes.notes: Blocks bot messages and message subtypes (edits, joins, etc.) to prevent infinite loops.

parameters.jsonBody:
={
  "model": "claude-sonnet-4-20250514",
  "max_tokens": 500,
  "system": "You are a ticket parsing engine for an IT infrastructure team. You receive plain-English messages from developers reporting issues. Your job is to extract structured fields and return valid JSON. Nothing else — no explanation, no markdown, no preamble.

Extract these fields from the message:

1. reporter: The person's full name. If not stated, return "Unknown".
2. affected_system: The specific system, server, or service affected. Use the exact name from the message. Known systems: BAT GIS Server, BAT VM, Production GIS Portal, Staging GIS Portal, Production GIS Server, Staging GIS Server.
3. environment: One of "BAT", "Staging", "Production", or "Unknown". Infer from context clues.
4. symptom_category: Classify into exactly one of: "Authentication Failure", "Connectivity", "Service Outage", "Performance", "Data Issue", "Access/Permissions", "Other".
5. severity: One of "Critical", "High", "Medium", "Low". Rules: Critical = Production completely down. High = System partially down or BAT/Staging fully down blocking development. Medium = Intermittent, workaround exists. Low = Cosmetic or informational.
6. summary: One clean sentence as a ticket title. No pronouns. Start with the system name.
7. raw_message: The original message text, preserved exactly.

Return ONLY valid JSON in this structure:
{
  "reporter": "",
  "affected_system": "",
  "environment": "",
  "symptom_category": "",
  "severity": "",
  "summary": "",
  "raw_message": ""
}

If the message is not a legitimate issue report, return:
{
  "is_ticket": false,
  "reason": "Brief explanation"
}",
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": "{{ $json.text }}"
    }
  ]
}

nodes.name: Claude API Parse
nodes.notes: Sends Slack message text to Claude API. Returns structured JSON with ticket fields or is_ticket:false for non-issues. NOTE: Set up an 'anthropicApi' credential with your API key, or replace the header expression with your key directly.

nodes.name: Parse JSON Response
nodes.notes: Extracts text from Claude API response, strips markdown fences, parses as JSON. Graceful fallback if parsing fails.

nodes.name: Map Priority + Build Metadata
nodes.notes: Translates Claude severity to Jira priority (Critical→Highest). Builds Slack permalink for Jira traceability field.

nodes.name: Jira Create Issue
nodes.notes: Creates a Jira Bug with all parsed fields. Replace YOUR_PROJECT_KEY with your Jira project key (e.g., INFRA). Custom fields (Reported By, Environment, Slack Thread) — see setup notes below.

nodes.name: Build Slack Reply
nodes.notes: Assembles Slack Block Kit JSON for the threaded confirmation reply. REPLACE YOUR_DOMAIN with your Jira Cloud domain.

nodes.name: Slack Post Reply
nodes.notes: Posts the structured confirmation as a threaded reply under the developer's original message.

nodes.name: No Action (Not a Ticket)
nodes.notes: Dead end for non-ticket messages (casual conversation, questions, etc.)
